personas.name: Booth/EMBA Framework Practitioner
personas.icp: EMBA / MBA candidate or recent alumni at Chicago Booth, NUS, SMU, INSEAD, IMD, HKUST or similar; uses CF / FS / OM / CS frameworks for both classroom case-studies and personal-investment idea generation; 30-45 years old; S$200k+ portfolio
pains: Damodaran XLS is excellent but slow — ten manual data-entries per ticker
ChatGPT narrative is fluent but ungrounded in framework + financial statements
Course-bundled spreadsheets stop updating after graduation
Wants reproducible, citable methodology to share with peers / classmates
personas.current_workaround: Damodaran XLS for DCF + WACC, ChatGPT for narrative, Yahoo Finance for raw data, manual reconciliation in Excel each weekend

nba.summary: Current stack: ChatGPT Plus (S$27/mo) + occasional Koyfin Plus (S$67/mo) + Damodaran XLS time cost (~3 hrs/mo at S$60/hr loaded = S$180/mo). Total spend + time S$274/mo. Market Tracker replaces ChatGPT-for-finance + Koyfin starter use.

implications.headline: Anchor MBA pricing on faculty-grade framework + reproducibility, not feature parity
implications.body: Persona pays for academic provenance and reproducibility, not for one-more-ratio. Pricing should anchor on Booth EMBA's perceived rigour — a S$39/mo tier that is 'methodology-cited' will out-convert a S$79 tier that is 'feature-equal to Koyfin'.

personas.name: SE-Asia Self-Directed Prosumer
personas.icp: 30-50 yr-old SG/MY/HK self-directed retail investor with S$50-500k portfolio; reads earnings calls; runs DIY screens; outgrew the broker-app summary cards; primary brokerage is moomoo / Tiger / IBKR
pains: Broker-app AI summary cards too shallow for actual conviction-building
Yahoo Finance / Investing.com don't surface valuation framework
Bloomberg / FactSet are out of reach financially
Want one place to read CF + FS + CS + OM lens on a name before sizing position
personas.current_workaround: moomoo / Tiger broker-app AI cards + Yahoo financials + Seeking Alpha Premium for narrative; manual cross-check in personal Notion

nba.summary: Direct comparable: Simply Wall St Premium SGD 22/mo (already paying close peer); moomoo/Tiger free fallback caps the upper tier. Market Tracker positions as 'Booth methodology vs Simply Wall St snowflake' at the same price band.

implications.headline: Competitive WTP ceiling is ~SGD 25/mo for SE-Asia prosumer
implications.body: Persona benchmarks against Simply Wall St SGD 22/mo. Pricing above SGD 30/mo for this segment requires materially differentiated framework or it loses on direct comparable. Use the Pro tier for this persona, not Team.

personas.name: Boutique Buy-Side / Family Office Analyst
personas.icp: Junior-mid analyst at a SG/HK family office or boutique fund (3-15 staff); annual fund AUM S$50M-S$500M; covers global equities; cannot justify a Bloomberg seat (~SGD 3.5k/mo) per analyst; uses Capital IQ/FactSet on a shared seat
pains: Shared seat queue creates 30-90 min waits for routine ratio pulls
Junior model-builds drink time that should go to memo writing
PM expects 'Booth-style' framework rigour in memos — has to write the lens manually
M&A target screens hit out-of-scope tickers regularly
personas.current_workaround: Bloomberg shared seat + Capital IQ Excel add-in + GuruFocus + occasional TIKR; takes ~6 hrs to compile a CF + FS + OM + CS one-pager per name

nba.summary: Junior-analyst loaded rate ~S$80/hr; saves 4 hrs per name analysis; runs 8 names per month = 32 hrs saved = S$2,560/mo opportunity-cost. Even Team tier at SGD 199/mo is < 8% of saved-time value.

implications.headline: Boutique-fund tier should be priced on time-saved, not feature count
implications.body: Persona is the highest-LTV segment by far. Team tier at SGD 199/mo (3 seats) is < 8% of opportunity-cost of saved analyst time — the bottleneck is procurement-friction not price. Lead with case-study time-saving in the pitch.

personas.name: SG SME CFO (Elitez channel cross-sell)
personas.icp: CFO / Finance Director at SG SME (50-300 headcount, S$10-100M revenue) inside the Elitez Group client base; uses framework analysis for personal portfolio + occasional board-pack capital-markets context; not buying enterprise tools
pains: Doesn't have time / inclination to learn TIKR / Koyfin chrome
Wants a 1-page Booth-style report to glance at over morning coffee
Resists firm-paid fintech tooling (treats it as personal expense)
Wants something more rigorous than ChatGPT but less effort than spreadsheets
personas.current_workaround: Yahoo Finance + WhatsApp tips from peers + DBS Vickers research PDFs + occasional ChatGPT

nba.summary: Personal-expense ceiling around S$30-50/mo. Persona benchmarks against the broker-research-bundle (free) + Seeking Alpha Premium SGD 32/mo. Market Tracker Pro at SGD 29/mo lands inside the 'I'd-pay-for-myself' band.

implications.headline: CFO cross-sell is volume-seeding via Elitez channel, not the GMV-engine
implications.body: Persona converts at SGD 29/mo personal — solid LTV but volume-seeding. Real revenue lift comes from leveraging this base for testimonials, EMBA referrals, and board-pack co-marketing with Elitez Pulse. Don't optimise this tier for ARPU; optimise for distribution + social proof.

personas.name: DIY ChatGPT-for-Finance User
personas.icp: Tech-savvy retail investor (ChatGPT Plus / Claude Pro user) who already pastes earnings transcripts into chat for narrative analysis; cost-conscious; values flexibility over polish
pains: AI hallucinates on equity data without source grounding
Re-prompting for the same framework template is friction
No persistence between conversations on a ticker
Wants 'finance-grounded ChatGPT' not 'another retail SaaS'
personas.current_workaround: ChatGPT Plus or Claude Pro + manual data paste + Yahoo Finance / Macrotrends; periodically wonders if Perplexity Finance is enough

nba.summary: Persona spends ~6 hrs/mo on DIY-ChatGPT analysis; 2 hrs of that is re-prompting / data-paste friction = S$120/mo time cost at S$60/hr. But the persona resists paying for tools because 'I can do it free.' Market Tracker free tier is the sole entry point; converts only on persistent value.

implications.headline: DIY-ChatGPT user converts on framework-grounded AI, not price
implications.body: Persona is the 'will I get hallucinations' worry-driven buyer. Free guest tier with rate limit + visible methodology lineage (model used, retrieval scope) is the conversion lever. Marketing copy must explicitly contrast 'grounded in the financial statements' vs ChatGPT's free-form output.

pricing_models.name: Free guest tier (rate-limited, S&P 500 only)
pricing_models.rationale: Current Streamlit app shape. Lowest-friction entry; converts ChatGPT-DIY worriers and cold prospects via the methodology page. Sets the floor on what the paid tier must beat.

pricing_models.name: Flat monthly Pro subscription (consumer SaaS norm)
pricing_models.rationale: Familiar shape (Simply Wall St, TIKR, Stock Rover, Seeking Alpha). Easy to model LTV; works for prosumer + EMBA + CFO bands. Loses the Booth-pricing differentiator if priced as commodity.

pricing_models.name: Annual EMBA-cohort site licence
pricing_models.rationale: School-paid bundle for an EMBA cohort (~50-150 students/yr per programme); lower per-seat price; high renewal stickiness; halo-marketing for retail. Aligns with how programmes already buy classroom XLS / case packs.

pricing_models.name: Team / firm tier (3-10 seats, boutique fund)
pricing_models.rationale: Multi-seat tier for a family office or boutique fund. Locks in higher ARPU; shared screening + watchlists; limited-vs-Bloomberg positioning. Requires SSO + admin controls before launch.

pricing_models.name: Hybrid: free + Pro + Team + EMBA-cohort site licence
pricing_models.rationale: Most defensible long-run shape. Free covers the brand + funnel; Pro covers the SE-Asia prosumer + CFO; Team covers boutique funds; EMBA-cohort is the halo-and-credibility lane.

elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Persona has S$200k+ portfolio; pays for tooling + spends 3+ hrs/mo on DIY analysis. WTP at S$39/mo is sub-1% of portfolio; methodology trust is the binding constraint, not price.
Persona benchmarks tightly against Simply Wall St (S$22/mo); every S$5/mo beyond S$25 sheds materially more deals. Free tier (broker apps) pulls hard at the low end.
Time-saving math means even S$199/mo is < 8% of saved-analyst-cost. Procurement friction (legal review, vendor onboarding) is the binding constraint.
Personal-expense ceiling around S$30-50/mo. Above that, persona retreats to broker app + Yahoo. Below S$20 not credible as a 'real tool'.
Persona resists paying for tools — 'I can do it free.' Free tier converts on framework-grounded value, not on price. Anything > S$0 needs a non-trivial methodology hook to convert.

recommended_tiers.name: Guest (free)
what_in: S&P 500 ticker lookup
All four Booth modules (CF / FS / OM / CS)
AI narrative on each module
PDF export of the analysis
Methodology page + lineage citations
what_excluded: Custom inputs (override risk-free rate, ERP, beta)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Try the Booth-EMBA framework on any S&P 500 stock. No login. No credit card.

recommended_tiers.name: Pro (consumer)
what_in: All Guest features
Full ticker scope: S&P 500 + SGX (STI) + HK + ASX
Watchlists + email alerts on framework score deltas
10-yr financial history
Custom inputs (risk-free rate / ERP / beta override)
PDF + DOCX export with branded cover
Saved analyses + revision history
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$29/mo. Less than your Spotify Family. The Booth methodology lens on every name you watch.

recommended_tiers.name: Team (boutique fund)
what_in: All Pro features for 3 seats
Shared watchlists + collaborative comments
Memo-generation: one-page Booth-style PDF per ticker
API access (REST) for ratio + narrative pulls
SSO / admin controls
Quarterly written framework refresh from the Market Tracker team
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$199/mo for 3 seats. Save 32 analyst-hours per month. Less than 8% of the time you'd otherwise pay junior analysts to compile.

recommended_tiers.name: EMBA Cohort Site Licence
what_in: All Pro features for 1 cohort (50-150 students)
Faculty admin dashboard
Classroom case-study templates aligned to CF / FS / OM / CS modules
Co-branded methodology page
1 onboarding workshop with Market Tracker team
Annual renewal at -10% if cohort > 80% conversion
what_excluded: Custom syllabus integrations beyond the four base modules
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Less per-seat than the cohort's Excel licence. Methodology stays available to alumni for life on the Pro tier.

grants.name: PSG (Productivity Solutions Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered SME (under 200 staff or under S$100M turnover); pre-approved IMDA solution. Market Tracker not currently PSG-listed but the SaaS-side admin tooling could be listed in Year-2 for the Team tier.

grants.name: SkillsFuture Credit (Singapore)
grants.eligibility: SG citizen / PR; reimbursable on accredited financial-literacy / valuation training. Pathway: bundle Pro tier with a SkillsFuture-accredited 'Booth methodology' workshop.
